channel.title: World
channel.description: The Washington Post World section provides information and analysis of breaking world news stories. In addition to our world news and video, Post World News offers discussions and blogs on major international news and economic issues.

item.title: U.S. moves toward troop reductions in Europe as Hegseth scolds NATO allies
item.dc:creator: Ellen Francis
item.description: The United States will review force levels and is immediately cutting the number of assets it would activate for the continent in a crisis.

item.title: First steps in peace deal demand far more from U.S. than Iran
item.dc:creator: Greg Miller
item.description: The memorandum of understanding requires numerous steps by Washington including lifting sanctions and freeing billions in frozen assets, while Iran was required only to open the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Ukraine pummels Russian capital with what may be largest drone strike yet on Moscow
item.dc:creator: Mary Ilyushina, Natalia Abbakumova
item.description: After weeks of intensifying bombing of Kyiv, Ukraine hit back with a swarm of drones, damaging buildings and igniting an oil refinery.

item.title: The local election that could topple Britain’s prime minister
item.dc:creator: Steve Hendrix
item.description: The outcome of a special election in the small working-class district of Makerfield could upend Labour Party leadership and U.K. politics.

item.title: Senate targets Hegseth’s travel in standoff over apparent Iran school attack, boat strikes
item.dc:creator: Tara Copp, Noah Robertson
item.description: Frustrated lawmakers are increasing pressure on the defense secretary to get answers they’ve sought for months.

item.title: Trump defends his Iran deal, criticizes longtime ally Israel as G-7 ends
item.dc:creator: Dan Diamond, Cleve R. Wootson Jr., Natalie Allison, Michael Birnbaum
item.description: In a sprawling news conference at the G-7 summit in France, the president touted the economic benefits of the ceasefire and threatened force if it fails.

item.title: Russia’s war in Ukraine comes back into Trump’s focus with no end in sight
item.dc:creator: Mary Ilyushina
item.description: The president met with Ukraine’s Volodymyr Zelensky at the G-7 summit but told reporters the conflict “has no impact on us … we’re thousands of miles away.”
